notes.completion_rate: 83% completion is solid for a 64-item sprint. 11 items moved forward.
notes.oos_total: 11 OOS additions indicate mid-sprint scope changes. Review planning accuracy and stakeholder alignment.
notes.rollover: 11 items (17%) rolled over from earlier Q sprints. Investigate blockers or sizing issues.
notes.cycle_time: 18d median cycle time is a red flag. Target under 7d. Identify bottlenecks in workflow.
notes.type_split: 39% bugs is borderline high. Monitor quality trends and consider root cause analysis if pattern continues.

notes.completion_rate: 90% completion is solid execution. Sprint closed with 45 of 50 items delivered.
notes.oos_total: 5 OOS items added mid-sprint indicates good scope discipline with minimal reactive work.
notes.rollover: 27 items rolled over (54%) is concerningly high—investigate sprint planning accuracy and capacity estimates for next quarter.
notes.cycle_time: 19d median cycle time is excessive and blocks throughput. Break down work into smaller units and identify bottlenecks causing multi-week delays.
notes.type_split: 40% bugs at upper acceptable threshold. Monitor defect trends—sustained bug volume may indicate tech debt or quality issues upstream.

notes.completion_rate: 78% completion is below target. 13 items incomplete suggests scope was too large or impediments slowed delivery.
notes.oos_total: 5 OOS items added mid-sprint. Low disruption indicates solid planning discipline.
notes.rollover: 24 rollover items (41% of sprint) is high. Review estimation accuracy and identify blockers causing multi-sprint carryover.
notes.cycle_time: 17d median cycle time is concerning—more than double healthy range. Investigate bottlenecks in review/testing or work breakdown size.
notes.type_split: 41% bugs indicates quality debt or insufficient testing upstream. Consider root cause analysis and preventive measures.

notes.completion_rate: 61% completion is below healthy threshold. 23 items incomplete despite sprint close suggests scope overload or estimation issues.
notes.oos_total: Only 3 items added mid-sprint shows strong planning discipline.
notes.rollover: 32 items (54%) carried from previous quarter sprint. High rollover indicates chronic overcommitment or blocked work.
notes.cycle_time: 16d median cycle time is concerning—more than double healthy range. Investigate bottlenecks in workflow.
notes.type_split: 32% bugs is within acceptable range, though near upper threshold. Monitor for upward trend.

notes.completion_rate: Mid-sprint at 33% complete — tracking below target pace for healthy 80%+ closure. Monitor velocity closely through second half.
notes.oos_total: Excellent sprint discipline with only 1 out-of-scope addition. Planning process holding strong.
notes.rollover: Critical: 26 items rolled over (51% of sprint) from prior Q1 sprints. High carryover indicates scope/capacity mismatch or blocked work requiring triage.
notes.cycle_time: 16d median cycle time is concerning — more than double healthy threshold. Investigate blockers, handoffs, or work breakdown size.
notes.type_split: Healthy type distribution at 20% bugs, 39% stories, 41% tasks. Balance supports feature delivery with maintenance work.

notes.completion_rate: Mid-sprint at 34% complete with 17/50 items done. Trajectory suggests completion rate may fall below healthy threshold—monitor closely through sprint end.
notes.oos_total: Only 2 items added post-planning shows strong scope discipline and minimal reactive work.
notes.rollover: 17 items rolled over (34% of sprint)—high carryover indicates planning misalignment or overcommitment from prior sprint.
notes.cycle_time: 7d median cycle time is at upper boundary of normal range. Average of 9.8d suggests some items experiencing delays—investigate blockers.
notes.type_split: Bug proportion at 24% is healthy and within normal range. Story/task balance appropriate for platform work.

notes.total: 164 tickets scoped for Q2 with 13 days remaining in the quarter (86% elapsed). Team is carrying 30 in-progress tickets into the final sprint; scope appears healthy given throughput.
notes.completed: 110 tickets closed through 5 completed sprints (67% completion rate). With one sprint remaining, the team is on track to reach ~75-80% completion by quarter-end if current velocity holds.
notes.completion_rate: 67% completion at 86% through the quarter signals slight underdelivery versus plan. Final sprint performance will determine whether the team closes the gap or finishes below target—watch for carryover into Q3.
notes.releases_shipped: 21 releases shipped across 5 closed sprints, averaging 4 releases per sprint. Healthy cadence maintained throughout the quarter with last release on 2026-06-11, consistent with web platform expectations for frequent deployment.
notes.oos_total: 20 out-of-sprint additions (12% of total) indicate generally disciplined sprint planning. All OOS items have been resolved—no outstanding unplanned debt.
notes.oos_open: All 20 out-of-sprint tickets have been resolved. Zero open OOS items demonstrates disciplined sprint planning and effective handling of reactive work.
notes.type_split: Bug ratio at 35% (58 bugs out of 164 tickets) is elevated and suggests ongoing quality issues requiring attention. Stories and tasks are balanced, but the bug volume warrants root-cause analysis to reduce reactive work.
notes.avg_releases: Median of 4 releases per sprint is strong and consistent for a web platform team. Sprint 98 shipped only 1 release (outlier), but other sprints maintained 4-6 releases, indicating healthy deployment rhythm.
notes.time_logged: 1,926 hours logged against 2,404 estimated (80% of planned capacity). Team is under-logging by ~478 hours, which may indicate tracking gaps or underutilisation. Flag for investigation to ensure accurate capacity planning.
notes.estimate_accuracy: 80% estimate accuracy with -20% variance (team is under-spending time relative to estimates). This is healthy—estimates are conservative and realistic. However, 12 tickets (7%) have no estimate, which is acceptable hygiene.
notes.no_estimate: 12 tickets (7% of scope) lack time estimates. This is low and reflects good planning discipline—minimal impact on forecast accuracy.
notes.rollover: 40 tickets rolled over across sprints (24% of total scope) reduces sprint predictability. Rollover was particularly high in sprints 95-97 (55-60% per sprint), though sprint 94 and 98 were lower. Pattern suggests intermittent capacity or scope estimation challenges.
notes.cycle_time: Median cycle time of 15 days is above target (7 days or less is healthy). Mean of 21 days indicates outlier tickets are inflating the average. This suggests blockers or long-running work that should be broken down or expedited.
notes.assignee_workload: Sandor Kocsis carries 29% of total tickets (47) with an 87% completion rate—workload is concentrated but well-executed. Dejan Kosanovic and Ivor Didovic have low completion rates (41% and 43% respectively) despite moderate ticket counts, indicating blockers or scope issues that need resolution. Three new team members joined mid-quarter (25 May); their contribution is minimal so far, as expected during onboarding.
